label: Azure
description: Connect to Microsoft Azure using a service principal to manage resources, query data, and automate cloud operations.

form.title: Connect Azure
form.subtitle: Authenticate with a service principal (app registration) to grant Anton scoped access to your Azure environment.

methods.label: Service Principal (Client Secret)
methods.description: Paste your Entra ID tenant ID, app registration client ID, and client secret. The simplest way to connect Azure — no OAuth browser flow required.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via Service Principal Client Secret

This method uses an Azure Entra ID (formerly Azure AD) app registration with a client secret. It is the recommended path for programmatic access.

## 1. Create an App Registration

1. Open the [Azure Portal](https://portal.azure.com) and search for **Microsoft Entra ID** (formerly Azure Active Directory).
2. Go to **App registrations → New registration**.
3. Give it a name like `Anton`, leave the default account type, and click **Register**.
4. On the app's **Overview** page, copy:
   - **Application (client) ID** → paste into **Client ID** below.
   - **Directory (tenant) ID** → paste into **Tenant ID** below.

## 2. Create a Client Secret

1. In the app registration, go to **Certificates & secrets → Client secrets → New client secret**.
2. Add a description (e.g. `Anton`) and choose an expiry. Click **Add**.
3. Copy the **Value** column immediately — it is only shown once.
4. Paste it into **Client Secret** below.

## 3. Assign a Role (optional but recommended)

1. Go to **Subscriptions** and open your target subscription.
2. Click **Access control (IAM) → Add role assignment**.
3. Pick a role (e.g. **Reader** for read-only, **Contributor** for write access).
4. Assign it to the app registration you just created.
5. Copy the **Subscription ID** from the subscription's Overview page and paste it below.

## Notes

- If your secret expires, generate a new one in the portal and update Anton — the old one will stop working.
- Prefer the **Reader** role unless Anton needs to create or modify resources.
- For multi-tenant setups, you can leave **Subscription ID** blank and Anton will list available subscriptions at runtime.

fields.name: tenant_id
fields.label: Tenant ID
fields.description: Your Azure Entra ID (Azure AD) Directory (tenant) ID. Found on the Entra ID Overview page or under App registrations → your app → Overview.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: Client ID
fields.description: The Application (client) ID of your app registration. Found on App registrations → your app → Overview.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: Client Secret
fields.description: The secret value created under Certificates & secrets → Client secrets. Shown only once — copy it immediately after creation.

fields.name: subscription_id
fields.label: Subscription ID
fields.description: Optional. Scopes Anton to a specific Azure subscription. Found on the Subscriptions page in the portal. Leave blank to access all subscriptions the service principal can see.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (Bring Your Own Client)
methods.description: Use the Microsoft identity platform OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow. Requires your own app registration client ID and client secret — a browser window opens for interactive sign-in.
methods.how_to:
## OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code Flow

This method opens a browser for interactive Microsoft sign-in. You must still supply your own app registration credentials (Anton does not ship a hosted client for Azure).

## 1. Create an App Registration

1. Open the [Azure Portal](https://portal.azure.com) → **Microsoft Entra ID → App registrations → New registration**.
2. Name it `Anton OAuth`, select **Accounts in this organizational directory only** (or multi-tenant if needed).
3. Under **Redirect URI**, select **Public client/native (mobile & desktop)** and enter `http://localhost`.
4. Click **Register**.
5. Copy the **Application (client) ID** and **Directory (tenant) ID** from the Overview page.

## 2. Add a Client Secret

1. Go to **Certificates & secrets → Client secrets → New client secret**.
2. Set a description and expiry, click **Add**, and copy the **Value** immediately.

## 3. Grant API Permissions

1. Go to **API permissions → Add a permission → Azure Service Management → user_impersonation**.
2. Click **Add permissions**. Grant admin consent if prompted.

## 4. Connect in Anton

1. Paste the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** into the fields below.
2. Click **Submit** — a browser window opens to Microsoft's sign-in page.
3. Sign in with your Azure account and approve the permissions.
4. Anton stores the refresh token locally for future use.

## Notes

- Tokens expire; Anton will refresh them automatically using the `offline_access` scope.
- To revoke access, visit [My Apps](https://myapps.microsoft.com) → the app → **Revoke permissions**.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Client ID
fields.description: Application (client) ID from your Entra ID app registration Overview page.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
fields.description: Client secret value from Certificates & secrets. Shown only once in the portal.

fields.name: tenant_id
fields.label: Tenant ID
fields.description: Optional. Leave blank to use the common endpoint (multi-tenant). Set this to restrict sign-in to a single directory.

fields.name: subscription_id
fields.label: Subscription ID
fields.description: Optional. Scopes Anton to a specific Azure subscription after authentication.
